app.title: Cost of Living Simulator
app.tagline: A year in Denver or Providence — ballpark numbers, in your currency, in your language.

report.title: Printable report
report.subtitle: A print-friendly summary for saving as PDF from the browser.

kids.title: Children
kids.subtitle: Add, remove, and configure every child in the household.
kids.name: Child name
kids.kidASubtitle: The one enrolled in Jewish day school.
kids.kidBSubtitle: The one with learning or personal difficulties.
kids.kidAFootnote: School, grant %, and aftercare are set in the Lifestyle tab.
kids.ageHint: Age affects food scaling and school grade.
kids.placementHint: Choose public, Jewish day school, self-pay private school, or district-funded special education.
kids.kidBHasIEP: Younger child has an IEP / special-ed plan
kids.iepHint: An IEP unlocks school-provided therapy and accommodations.
kids.kidBHasWaiver: Younger child has a Medicaid disability waiver
kids.waiverHint: Katie Beckett / TEFRA / HCBS: Medicaid granted on disability alone, independent of parent income.

food.title: Food cost multiplier
food.subtitle: One knob to adjust the monthly grocery total — kosher, dietary needs, or just how expensive your shopping is.
food.multiplierHint: Applied directly to the USDA moderate food plan for a family of 4. 1.00 = plain baseline, 1.30 ≈ typical kosher premium, higher for specialty diets.
food.caption: City kosher default ≈ ×{cityMult}. Use ×1.00 for a plain USDA baseline.

expenses.title: Custom expenses
expenses.subtitle: Turn the old miscellaneous bucket into editable line items.
expenses.name: Expense name

lifestyle.title: Setup
lifestyle.subtitle: Who lives here, where they live, what they earn, and the big monthly line items.

city.title: City
city.label: Where they live
city.hint: Drives rent, utilities, state tax, car insurance, ACA benchmark, and kosher grocery multiplier.

parentA.employedHint: If off, Parent A has no salary this year.
parentA.jobHint: Affects salary range suggestions only; doesn't change tax math.
parentA.salaryHint: Before tax. Drag to simulate raises, furloughs or career changes.
parentA.employerHealthHint: If on, the household can take the employer plan instead of the ACA marketplace.
parentA.employerPremiumHint: What the paycheck deduction would total for the year.

parentB.modeHint: Determines which benefits and work limits apply.
parentB.salaryHint: For SSDI, any earnings count toward the SGA limit below.
parentB.ssdiMonthlyHint: Based on past work history — typical 2025 family average is about $1,580.
parentB.ssiMonthlyHint: Federal maximum 2025 is $967/mo for a single recipient — reduced by other income.
parentB.sgaWarn: Earning above ${sga}/mo can suspend SSDI (Substantial Gainful Activity).

housing.title: Housing
housing.bedroomsHint: Drives the default rent from the city median.
housing.tierHint: A quick way to pick a cheaper or nicer apartment without typing a number. Budget ≈ 85% of city median, Premium ≈ 120%.
housing.rentHint: Default is the city median for this bedroom count and tier, bumped ~15% for walkable/Jewish neighborhoods.

transport.title: Transport
transport.hasCarHint: Turns on insurance, gas and maintenance costs for one car (city-average numbers).
transport.carTypeHint: Used cars: no loan payment but maintenance runs higher. New cars: adds a monthly loan payment but fewer repairs. Minivans cost more to insure and fuel; compacts less.
transport.usesTransitHint: Adds one monthly transit pass for the household.
transport.noCarNote: No car — only transit and walking.

kidA.heading: Older child — Jewish day school
kidA.schoolHint: Sticker price varies by grade; grant % is applied on top.
kidA.grantHint: Estimate from income; schools typically cover 0–80% for families in this range.

kidB.heading: Younger child
kidB.placementHint: Public placements are tuition-free; district-funded private placements require IEP advocacy.
kidB.therapyHint: After insurance; OT / speech / ABA gap that parents cover.
kidB.tuitionHint: Only applies if the placement is self-pay.

health.title: Health insurance
health.strategyHint: The path the family takes to stay covered.

feasibility.green: Sustainable — surplus over two months of expenses.
feasibility.red: Deficit — expenses exceed income this year.

master.title: The master equation
master.formula: Net = Gross income + Assistance − Taxes − Expenses

explain.heading: How each number is built
explain.subtitle: Tap any row to see the live formula.
explain.housingDesc: Rent × 12 + utilities + renters insurance. Tier multiplier applied to city median.
explain.housingFormula: rent {rent}/mo ({tier}, ×{tierMult} of median {median}) × 12 + utilities {utilities} + renters ins. {insurance} = {total}
explain.taxesDesc: Federal + FICA + state + local, minus refundable CTC/EITC.
explain.taxesCreditsNote: Refundable credits: CTC {ctc} + EITC {eitc} + state EITC {stateEitc} — added back via Assistance.
explain.healthDesc: ACA benchmark minus PTC subsidy, plus expected out-of-pocket.
explain.schoolsDesc: School, tuition, support, and therapy costs across all children.
explain.foodDesc: USDA moderate plan × kosher multiplier (if on).
explain.transportDesc: Car costs (if owned) plus optional transit pass.
explain.otherDesc: Editable user-managed expenses, grouped by category.
explain.assistanceDesc: Money flowing back into the household.
explain.snapIneligible: SNAP not eligible at this income.

cliff.title: The cliff chart
cliff.subtitle: Net cash at each level of household earned income — watch for the drops where benefits phase out.

compare.title: Side by side
compare.subtitle: The same family, the other city.

assumptions.title: Assumptions
assumptions.subtitle: Open any row to read what it changes.
assumptions.acaExtendedHint: If off, pre-IRA rules apply: 400% FPL cliff returns and contribution caps rise.
assumptions.kosherMultiplierHint: Applied to the USDA moderate-cost food plan when 'keeps kosher' is on.
assumptions.reserveHint: Surplus must cover this many months of total expenses to be considered sustainable.

schools.jcdsri: Jewish Community Day School of RI
